title: MiFID II Conflicts of Interest — Research Analyst vs Trading Desk Information Barrier Violations

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), DLP sourcetype
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload IN ("MicrosoftTeams","SharePoint") earliest=-30d
| lookup research_wall_groups.csv user_id as UserId OUTPUT wall_side
| stats values(wall_side) as sides by Operation, ObjectId
| where mvcount(sides)>1
| stats count

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), DLP sourcetype.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Operation, UserId, Workload); `research_wall_groups.csv` (user_id, wall_side).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate `research_wall_groups.csv` from HR; (2) tune ObjectId granularity; (3) heavy false positive review—use as triage; (4) integrate with eComms archive; (5) document controls in conflicts register.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload IN ("MicrosoftTeams","SharePoint") earliest=-30d
| lookup research_wall_groups.csv user_id as UserId OUTPUT wall_side
| stats values(wall_side) as sides by Operation, ObjectId
| where mvcount(sides)>1
| stats count
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Conflicts of Interest — Research Analyst vs Trading Desk Information Barrier Violations** — Chinese walls protect research independence. Detecting shared workspace file activity, IM channels, or email between restricted research and trading groups supports conflicts management beyond policy PDFs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Operation, UserId, Workload); `research_wall_groups.csv` (user_id, wall_side). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), DLP sourcetype. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="ms:o365:management", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by Operation, ObjectId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvcount(sides)>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the DLP or Microsoft 365 compliance center and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console for the same window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (shared objects), Single value (incidents), Bar by Workload.
grandmaExplanation: We chinese walls protect research independence.
